chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI CP No.D-02 of 2022 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For hearing of Misc. No.12/2022 (stay) 2. For hearing of Main Case 19/01/2022: Mr. Abdul Qayyum Abbasi, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, Advocate for Respondent. Mr. Kafeel Ahmed Abbasi, D.A.G. -.-.-.-.-.- Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that instant petition can be disposed of in terms of earlier orders already passed by this Court in number of petitions under similar circumstances, whereby the respondents have been restrained from adopting any coercive measures for enforcing recovery of the impugned demand, which is subject matter of appeal pending before Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, till its final decision. In support of his contention learned counsel has placed photocopy of the order dated 04.12.2017 passed by this Court in C.P. No.D-2525/2017. Learned Deputy Attorney General in view of earlier orders already passed by this Court, does not oppose the disposal of instant Petition in the above terms. Accordingly, instant petition is being disposed of with the directions to the respondents not to enforce the recovery of impugned demand, which is subject matter of appeal before Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, till its final decision. It is expected that Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, may decide the appeal of the petitioner, preferably within a period of one month from the date of receipt of this order, which shall be communicated to the concerned Commissioner by the petitioner within seven days of this order and till then the respondents shall not enforce the recovery of impugned demand from the petitioner.

Accordingly, instant petition is being disposed of with the directions to the respondents not to enforce the recovery of impugned demand, which is subject matter of appeal before Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, till its final decision. It is expected that Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, may decide the appeal of the petitioner, preferably within a period of one month from the date of receipt of this order, which shall be communicated to the concerned Commissioner by the petitioner within seven days of this order and till then the respondents shall not enforce the recovery of impugned demand from the petitioner. However, if an adverse order is passed by the Commissioner, Inland Revenue, (Appeals-I), Karachi, against the petitioner the respondents shall not enforce recovery of the impugned demand for another period of seven days to enable the petitioner to seek further remedy in accordance with law. Petition stands disposed of in the above terms alongwith all the listed and pending application(s), if any. JUDGE JUDGE SM
